questions.question: Why is the 'Trust Us, We're Nice' reveal pattern problematic for NFT launches?
options: It requires too much gas to execute
Someone knows which token IDs map to rare traits before the public mint completes
questions.correctAnswer: Someone knows which token IDs map to rare traits before the public mint completes

questions.question: What's the main vulnerability of using block.timestamp for NFT trait randomness?
options: Block timestamps are always the same
Validators can see the result before finalizing the block and sophisticated users can simulate outcomes locally
Block timestamps don't exist on all chains
questions.correctAnswer: Validators can see the result before finalizing the block and sophisticated users can simulate outcomes locally

questions.question: In threshold randomness for NFTs, the mint transaction and trait assignment happen in the same transaction.
What makes threshold randomness more secure than centralized oracles for NFT traits?
options: An attacker must compromise multiple independent operators meeting the threshold, not just one service
questions.correctAnswer: An attacker must compromise multiple independent operators meeting the threshold, not just one service

questions.question: The gap between the mint transaction and the randomness callback is what makes NFT traits ______.
Why do we divide the random value before taking modulo when deriving multiple traits?
options: To make the code run faster
To prevent correlation between traits by using different parts of the random value
To make the traits more rare
questions.correctAnswer: To prevent correlation between traits by using different parts of the random value

questions.question: With threshold randomness, the project team can control which wallets receive rare NFT traits.
